findings: TDD RED: added a 6-assertion 'status --all' test group to scripts/test-keel-state.cjs (before the revert-check block); pre-implementation run failed 6/6 with 'code=1 out=FAIL: no manifest for story --all — pipeline not initialized (run: init --all)' — proving --all currently falls through to cmdStatus/readManifest.

TDD GREEN: implemented cmdStatusAll() in scripts/keel-state.cjs exactly per docs/design/KEEL-102-design.md §1.1 (readdirSync withFileTypes, manifest.json-only dirs, .lock/*.tmp/'--all' skipped, local try/catch skip-and-mark, localeCompare sort, JSON.stringify 2-space, die only when the state root itself is unreadable); full suite now 21/21 passed.

Dispatcher: single intercept line `if (cmd === 'status' && storyId === '--all') { cmdStatusAll(); process.exit(0); }` inserted after the !storyId guard and before the switch, mirroring the memory-check early-exit idiom; '--all' is truthy so the guard needed no change (design §1.2).

Test coverage maps to the design test plan: (a) two-story fixture — FLEET-A feature advanced to phase 2 via gate PASS, FLEET-B defect halted via 3 gate FAILs — asserts exit 0, JSON array length 2, sort order, field values, and exact 4-key projection {story_id, scope, current_phase, halted}; (b) no .keel/state prints [] exit 0; (c) corrupt '{invalid' manifest yields {story_id, error} while the healthy sibling GOOD-1 is still listed, exit 0; (d) single-story deep view pinned (B-10/AC-3).

Live run in the repo root: `node scripts/keel-state.cjs status --all` printed a sorted 2-entry array — KEEL-101 {scope defect, current_phase 7, halted false} and KEEL-102 {scope feature, current_phase 4, halted false} — exit 0.

AC-3 structural guard verified via git diff: cmdStatus, its switch case, and every helper are byte-for-byte untouched; the only engine edits are the new function, the one dispatcher line, and the two usage strings (header comment line 20 and USAGE const) per design §1.2.

Skip-and-mark deliberately does NOT reuse readJson()/readManifest() (both die(1) and would abort the sweep, violating B-5); a code comment records this so a future refactor cannot regress it. Sweep is lock-free — safe because writeManifest is tmp+renameSync atomic (ADR-001).

Self code review: cmdStatusAll body is 26 lines (<30 cap), no debug leftovers, no TODOs, no new dependencies, no hard-coded filesystem paths in user-facing strings beyond the already-interpolated root in the B-9 die message (lesson L-1).
Pre-existing unrelated working-tree modification noted: bin/package-plugin.sh (Windows zip fallback) was already dirty before phase 4 and was not touched by this change.
This is a feature (scope: feature), not a defect — no RCA/revert-check step applies; regression safety is carried by the full 21/21 engine suite including the pinned single-story status contract.
decisions: Implemented the design verbatim — no redesign; cmdStatusAll placed directly after cmdStatus, dispatcher intercept as a single early-exit line
Test fixture halts the defect story through the real engine path (3 gate FAILs) rather than hand-editing manifest.halted, so the test exercises production halt behavior
Updated both usage surfaces (header comment and USAGE const) to advertise status --all, as design §1.2 requires
Left the unrelated pre-existing bin/package-plugin.sh working-tree change untouched (out of scope for KEEL-102)
